title: thymus serpyllum var. conglomeratus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. conglomeratus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family and native to Europe and Mediterranean regions. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of wild thyme contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, and is also used in culinary and cosmetic applications.
